_sources.fact: Christmas Town USA tradition started in 1956 by McAdenville Men's Club and Pharr's founder; draws 600,000+ visitors during 26-day December festival
McAden Mills chartered 1881, became largest mill in Gaston County; Thomas Edison built hydroelectric generator for mill in 1883
William Pharr purchased mill operation and transformed it into Pharr Yarns, major textile producer; mill village undergoing revitalization

localP1: McAdenville is a historic textile mill village in the heart of Gaston County. McAden Mills, chartered in 1881, grew into one of the county's most innovative operations, and Thomas Edison built its hydroelectric generator in 1883. Under William Pharr, it transformed into Pharr Yarns, and today the village is nationally known as Christmas Town USA, a 26-day lights festival born in 1956 that draws over 600,000 visitors each December.
localP2: We build for the people who make McAdenville work: the shop owner on Main Street, the restaurant welcoming holiday visitors, the service businesses keeping the mill village alive year-round. Every site is designed around your business, whether you're built on decades of textile history or riding the wave of seasonal tourism.
